docs.text: On 1990-08-02, a hormuz-type disruption occurred with severity 0.3 lasting 180 days. Brent crude moved +12.0% on Day 1, +28.0% by Day 7, and +45.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -8.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.80%, power-sector stress reached 55/100, and SPR drawdown was +2.10 days-equivalent.

On 2003-03-20, a hormuz-type disruption occurred with severity 0.2 lasting 90 days. Brent crude moved +5.0% on Day 1, +12.0% by Day 7, and +18.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -4.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.30%, power-sector stress reached 35/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.80 days-equivalent.

On 2005-08-29, a opec-type disruption occurred with severity 0.15 lasting 60 days. Brent crude moved +4.0% on Day 1, +8.0% by Day 7, and +10.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -3.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.20%, power-sector stress reached 28/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.50 days-equivalent.

On 2011-02-17, a opec-type disruption occurred with severity 0.25 lasting 240 days. Brent crude moved +3.0% on Day 1, +10.0% by Day 7, and +15.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -5.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.40%, power-sector stress reached 40/100, and SPR drawdown was +1.00 days-equivalent.

Refinery run rates changed -3.5%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.30%, power-sector stress reached 32/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.70 days-equivalent.

Refinery run rates changed -2.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.25%, power-sector stress reached 30/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.40 days-equivalent.

Refinery run rates changed -12.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.15%, power-sector stress reached 62/100, and SPR drawdown was +1.50 days-equivalent.

On 2019-06-13, a hormuz-type disruption occurred with severity 0.3 lasting 60 days. Brent crude moved +3.0% on Day 1, +5.0% by Day 7, and +4.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -2.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.10%, power-sector stress reached 25/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.30 days-equivalent.

On 2020-03-11, a opec-type disruption occurred with severity 0.3 lasting 90 days. Brent crude moved -10.0% on Day 1, -25.0% by Day 7, and -55.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -20.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -5.00%, power-sector stress reached 15/100, and SPR drawdown was -1.00 days-equivalent.

On 2020-03-08, a opec-type disruption occurred with severity 0.2 lasting 30 days. Brent crude moved -25.0% on Day 1, -30.0% by Day 7, and -45.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -8.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.80%, power-sector stress reached 20/100, and SPR drawdown was -0.50 days-equivalent.

On 2021-03-23, a red sea-type disruption occurred with severity 1.0 lasting 6 days. Brent crude moved +2.0% on Day 1, +4.0% by Day 7, and +1.5% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -1.5%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.05%, power-sector stress reached 18/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.20 days-equivalent.

On 2022-02-24, a opec-type disruption occurred with severity 0.25 lasting 365 days. Brent crude moved +8.0% on Day 1, +15.0% by Day 7, and +17.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -6.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.50%, power-sector stress reached 55/100, and SPR drawdown was +1.80 days-equivalent.

On 2022-10-05, a opec-type disruption occurred with severity 0.2 lasting 180 days. Brent crude moved +4.0% on Day 1, +6.0% by Day 7, and +8.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -2.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.20%, power-sector stress reached 28/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.50 days-equivalent.

On 2023-12-19, a red sea-type disruption occurred with severity 0.8 lasting 180 days. Brent crude moved +1.5% on Day 1, +3.0% by Day 7, and +4.5% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -3.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.15%, power-sector stress reached 35/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.60 days-equivalent.

On 2024-04-13, a hormuz-type disruption occurred with severity 0.4 lasting 14 days. Brent crude moved +3.5% on Day 1, +4.0% by Day 7, and +1.5% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -2.5%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.10%, power-sector stress reached 30/100, and SPR drawdown was +0.40 days-equivalent.

On 2025-01-15, a hormuz-type disruption occurred with severity 0.5 lasting 30 days. Brent crude moved +8.0% on Day 1, +12.0% by Day 7, and +6.0% by Day 30. Refinery run rates changed -8.0%, India's GDP impact was estimated at -0.20%, power-sector stress reached 55/100, and SPR drawdown was +1.20 days-equivalent.

IMF Working Paper 22/58 estimates that a 10 USD/barrel rise in Brent crude raises India's consumer price inflation by approximately 0.15 percentage points and reduces India's GDP growth by approximately 0.12 percentage points, reflecting India's high crude import dependency and the pass-through of energy costs into transport, manufacturing input costs, and fertiliser production.

The Reserve Bank of India's 2023 Monetary Policy Report identifies fuel and transport cost pass-through as a primary channel through which crude oil price shocks propagate into headline inflation in India, with petrol and diesel retail pricing carrying an estimated 0.65 and 0.58 passthrough coefficient respectively against international crude price movements.

PPAC (Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell) publishes India's monthly crude import statistics, showing approximately 88% import dependency, with the Strait of Hormuz corridor carrying roughly 40-45% of import volume from Gulf suppliers including Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Kuwait, and the Red Sea / Bab-el-Mandeb corridor carrying approximately 18% via Suez-side and East African routes.

The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow chokepoint between Iran and Oman connecting the Persian Gulf to the Arabian Sea. It is the world's most important oil chokepoint by volume and has repeatedly been the site of tanker seizures, naval posturing, and direct attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure, most notably the 2019 Abqaiq-Khurais attack on Saudi processing facilities. India sources a large share of its Gulf crude — from Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Kuwait — through this corridor.

The Red Sea and Bab-el-Mandeb corridor connects the Gulf of Aden to the Suez Canal and Mediterranean, providing the shortest sea route between Asia and Europe. Sustained Houthi attacks on commercial shipping from late 2023 forced a large share of global tanker traffic to reroute around the Cape of Good Hope, adding 12-14 days of transit time and raising freight and insurance costs on this route.

India's Strategic Petroleum Reserve is held at three underground sites — Visakhapatnam, Mangalore, and Padur — with a combined capacity of approximately 5.33 million metric tonnes, providing roughly 9.5 days of national crude consumption cover in an emergency, intended to bridge short-term supply disruptions while alternative sourcing or diplomatic resolution is pursued.
